Ā Y Ā T
A galaxy of 114 suns — every star a surah, bearing its name. Dive into any of them and its verses surround you as rings of light. The Quran rendered as a universe of signs — every one of its 6,236 verses a star, its 114 surahs the clusters of a galaxy, and 199 concepts drawn as constellations across the whole revelation. Arabic · English · اردو · हिन्दी

آياتĀYĀT
every star is a surah — click one to dive inside it
text sources & method

ĀYĀT · آيات

Every verse of the Quran is a star. The 114 surahs form clusters along the galaxy's spiral — warm gold for Meccan, cool blue for Medinan — and each cluster's stars follow the golden-angle phyllotaxis, in verse order from its centre outward. Selecting a topic ignites its verses across the entire galaxy and threads them, in mushaf order, into a constellation.

Text. Arabic (Uthmani): Tanzil-verified text via the quran-json project. English: Saheeh International (Umm Muhammad). Urdu: Fateh Muhammad Jalandhry. Hindi: Suhel Farooq Khan & Saifur Rahman Nadwi. Texts are reproduced unmodified; all rights with their scholars and Tanzil.net.

Topic index — method & honesty. The 199 topics were built by root-form concordance against the actual Arabic corpus (e.g. Tawakkul gathers every verse containing a و-ك-ل devotional form), supplemented by curated ranges for narratives. Machine-gathering by root is complete but not infallible — a root can serve more than one sense — so treat the index as a study companion, not a fatwa. Download the topic index (JSON) to review or refine it.

Built with three.js · single self-contained file · works offline once loaded.
